name: plan-history-schema
title: Add plan history table and DB layer for tracking completed plan snapshots
prompt:
In the ClaudeCode Orchestrator (src/orchestrator/), extend the database to store plan execution history.

1. In database.py, add a new table `plan_history`:
   ```sql
   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS plan_history (
       id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
       plan_id TEXT NOT NULL,
       snapshot_name TEXT NOT NULL,
       snapshot_at TEXT NOT NULL,
       summary TEXT,
       steps_json TEXT NOT NULL,
       FOREIGN KEY (plan_id) REFERENCES plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
   );
   ```
   `steps_json` stores a JSON array of all steps with their results/status at the time of the snapshot.

2. Add a `parent_plan_id` column to the `plans` table (nullable TEXT, FK to plans.id). This tracks lineage — which plan a new plan was derived from. Use ALTER TABLE if the column doesn't exist (wrap in try/except for idempotency).

3. In models.py, add a `PlanHistory` dataclass:
   - id (str/uuid), plan_id, snapshot_name, snapshot_at (ISO timestamp), summary (optional str), steps_json (str)

4. Add a `parent_plan_id: Optional[str] = None` field to the existing `Plan` dataclass.

5. In database.py, add CRUD methods:
   - `create_history_snapshot(plan_id, snapshot_name, summary=None) -> PlanHistory`: Reads all steps for the plan, serializes them (including results) to JSON, inserts a plan_history row.
   - `get_history_for_plan(plan_id) -> List[PlanHistory]`: Returns all snapshots for a plan, ordered by snapshot_at.
   - `get_full_lineage_history(plan_id) -> List[PlanHistory]`: Walks up parent_plan_id chain and returns history for the entire lineage.
   - `delete_history_snapshot(snapshot_id)`: Deletes a single snapshot.
   - Update `create_plan` and `get_plan`/`get_plans` to handle the new `parent_plan_id` column.

6. In services/orchestrator.py, after a full queue execution completes (all steps done), automatically create a history snapshot named 'Auto-snapshot after queue run' with a summary of how many steps succeeded/failed.

7. Add tests in tests/test_database.py for the new history methods: create snapshot, retrieve history, lineage traversal, cascading delete when plan is deleted.

Make sure all existing tests still pass. Commit.

name: history-viewer-ui
title: Build a history viewer panel in the UI for browsing plan execution snapshots
prompt:
In the ClaudeCode Orchestrator UI, add a history viewer so users can browse and manage plan history.

1. Create src/orchestrator/ui/history_viewer.py (a Toplevel window):
   - Takes a plan_id and db reference.
   - Shows a ttk.Treeview listing all PlanHistory snapshots for this plan: Snapshot Name, Date, Summary.
   - Below the Treeview, a PanedWindow with:
     - Left: a Treeview showing the steps from the selected snapshot (parsed from steps_json): #, Name, Title, Status.
     - Right: a read-only Text widget showing the full result of the selected step in the snapshot.
   - Buttons: 'Delete Snapshot', 'Create Snapshot Now' (manual snapshot with user-provided name/summary).
   - A 'View Full Lineage' checkbox that, when checked, also shows snapshots from parent plans (uses get_full_lineage_history). Group by plan name in the Treeview.

2. In main.py:
   - Add a 'History' button in the plan management area (left pane), enabled when a plan is selected.
   - Add 'View History' to the plan right-click context menu if one exists, or add a right-click menu on the plan Listbox.
   - Add Edit > 'Create Snapshot' menu item that creates a manual snapshot for the current plan.

3. In the step queue right-click context menu, add 'View History for This Step' — opens the history viewer and auto-selects the most recent snapshot, scrolling to that step.

4. Make sure the history viewer updates if the user creates or deletes a snapshot while it's open.

Commit.

name: history-context-for-agents
title: Inject plan history as context so each executing agent can see prior plan runs
prompt:
Extend the context sharing system so that executing agents receive plan history as part of their prompt context.

1. In src/orchestrator/services/context_builder.py, add a new function:
   `build_history_context(db, plan_id, max_chars=5000) -> str`
   - Load all history snapshots for the plan (and optionally its lineage via parent_plan_id).
   - For each snapshot, format a summary section:
     ```
     PLAN HISTORY:
     ===
     Snapshot: "Initial implementation" (2026-04-01)
     Summary: 10 steps succeeded, 0 failed
     Key results:
     - Step 1 (setup-project): Created project structure... [truncated]
     - Step 2 (add-models): Added dataclasses for Plan, PlanStep...
     ...
     ===
     ```
   - Truncate intelligently: most recent snapshot gets more space, older ones get just the summary line.
   - If the plan has a parent, include a brief lineage section: 'This plan continues from: <parent plan name>. Parent had N snapshots.'

2. In services/orchestrator.py, when building the full prompt:
   - If config.include_context is True, also call build_history_context and prepend it before the step context.
   - The final prompt structure becomes: history context + step context + 'TASK:\n' + step prompt + VERIFY_SUFFIX.

3. In config.py, add a new setting `include_history_context` (default: True) to allow users to toggle this separately from step context.

4. In ui/settings_dialog.py, add a checkbox for 'Include Plan History in Agent Context'.

5. In the step editor's 'Preview Full Prompt' (if it exists), update it to also show the history context section.

6. Add tests in tests/test_context_builder.py for build_history_context: test with no history, one snapshot, multiple snapshots, lineage history, and truncation.

Commit.

name: history-tool-use
title: Provide a history lookup tool that agents can invoke during execution
prompt:
Create a mechanism so that Claude agents executing inside the orchestrator can look up plan history on demand, rather than just getting it injected as static context.

1. Create src/orchestrator/services/history_tool.py:
   - This module writes a temporary CLAUDE.md (or appends to an existing one) in the plan's project_root before each step executes.
   - The injected section tells the agent about a history file it can read:
     ```
     ## Plan History
     This project has execution history from the orchestrator.
     Read the file `.orchestrator-history.json` in this directory for full plan history.
     It contains previous plan snapshots with step results that may help you understand context.
     ```
   - Function `write_history_file(db, plan_id, project_root)`: Writes `.orchestrator-history.json` to the project root containing:
     - Current plan info (name, steps, statuses)
     - All history snapshots for this plan and its lineage
     - Each snapshot includes step names, titles, and results
   - Function `cleanup_history_file(project_root)`: Removes `.orchestrator-history.json` after execution.
   - Function `inject_claude_md_hint(project_root)`: Appends the history hint to CLAUDE.md (creating it if needed). Tracks what was appended so it can be cleaned up.
   - Function `cleanup_claude_md_hint(project_root)`: Removes the appended section from CLAUDE.md (or deletes the file if it was created by us).

2. In services/orchestrator.py:
   - Before executing the first step of a queue run, call write_history_file and inject_claude_md_hint.
   - After execution completes (or is cancelled), call the cleanup functions.
   - Also call write_history_file before single-step execution.

3. Add `.orchestrator-history.json` to the project's .gitignore hint (mention in the injected CLAUDE.md section that agents should not commit this file).

4. In config.py, add `enable_history_tool` (default: True).

5. In ui/settings_dialog.py, add a checkbox for 'Write history file for agents'.

Commit.

name: amend-extend-plan
title: Allow plans to be amended and extended with new steps after execution
prompt:
Make plans amendable and extendable so users can add more steps to a completed or partially completed plan and continue execution.

1. In main.py, update the UI to support plan amendment:
   - After a plan finishes executing (some steps succeeded), the user should be able to add new steps and run them. Currently this should work, but make the UX explicit:
   - Add an 'Extend Plan' button (or menu item under Edit) that opens a dialog similar to NewPlanDialog but pre-filled with the current plan context.
   - The dialog shows:
     - The plan name (read-only) and project path.
     - A read-only summary of existing steps and their statuses (compact Treeview).
     - A text area where the user describes what they want to add/change next.
     - A 'Generate Steps' button that calls Claude with a prompt that includes:
       - The existing plan's step names, titles, and result summaries.
       - The plan's history snapshots.
       - The user's new description.
       - Instructions to generate only the NEW steps needed, numbered continuing from the last step.
     - Preview of generated steps.
     - 'Add to Plan' button that appends the new steps to the existing plan (with queue_positions continuing from the last step).

2. Create src/orchestrator/ui/extend_plan_dialog.py for this dialog.

3. In the main UI:
   - Add 'Extend Plan' to the Edit menu and as a button near the plan list.
   - Right-click on a plan should also show 'Extend Plan'.
   - Before extending, auto-create a history snapshot so the state before extension is preserved.

4. When new steps are appended, set their status to 'pending'. Existing succeeded steps remain unchanged. The user can then click 'Run Queue' to execute only the new pending steps.

5. In the step queue Treeview, visually separate the 'completed' section from the 'new/pending' section with a subtle divider or different background shade.

Commit.

name: derive-new-plan-from-old
title: Create new plans derived from existing ones using history as context
prompt:
Implement the ability to create a new plan that builds upon a completed plan, carrying forward its history and context.

1. Create src/orchestrator/ui/derive_plan_dialog.py (a Toplevel window):
   - Source plan selector (dropdown of all plans, defaulting to the currently selected plan).
   - Shows a read-only summary of the source plan: step count, status breakdown, last snapshot date.
   - Plan name Entry (auto-suggested: '<source plan name> - continued').
   - Project path Entry (defaults to source plan's project_root, with Browse button).
   - Option checkboxes:
     - 'Copy succeeded steps as reference (read-only)' — includes the old steps in the new plan marked as a special status (e.g. 'reference') so the user can see them but they won't re-execute.
     - 'Include full history from source plan' — links parent_plan_id so all lineage history is available.
   - Large text area for describing the new goals.
   - 'Generate Steps' button that calls Claude with:
     - Full history context from the source plan (snapshots + step results).
     - The user's new goals.
     - Instructions to generate steps that build upon the previous work.
   - Step preview Treeview.
   - 'Create Plan' button.

2. In models.py, add a new StepStatus value: `REFERENCE = "reference"` for steps carried over from a parent plan. These are display-only and never executed.

3. In main.py:
   - Add 'New Plan from Existing...' to the File menu.
   - Add it to the plan list right-click context menu.
   - When derived plan is created, set its parent_plan_id to the source plan's id.

4. Update the step queue color coding: reference steps get a distinct color (e.g. light purple #F3E5F5) and are shown above the pending steps.

5. The execution engine should skip steps with status=REFERENCE (treat like SKIPPED but don't change their status).

6. In the plan list (left pane), show lineage info: if a plan has a parent, display a small indicator like '↳ derived from <parent name>'.

Commit.

name: history-diff-and-compare
title: Add snapshot comparison and diff view for plan history
prompt:
Add the ability to compare two history snapshots side by side, so users can see how a plan evolved.

1. In src/orchestrator/ui/history_viewer.py, add a 'Compare' button:
   - The user selects two snapshots (multi-select in the Treeview, or a 'Compare with...' dropdown after selecting one).
   - Opens a new Toplevel: SnapshotDiffDialog.

2. Create src/orchestrator/ui/snapshot_diff_dialog.py:
   - Side-by-side layout (two PanedWindow columns).
   - Left column: Snapshot A steps (Treeview + result viewer).
   - Right column: Snapshot B steps (Treeview + result viewer).
   - Between them, a summary panel:
     - Steps added in B that weren't in A.
     - Steps whose status changed (e.g. pending → succeeded).
     - Steps whose results differ.
   - Color-code the differences: green for new/improved steps, red for removed/regressed, yellow for changed.

3. In the history viewer, add a 'Timeline' view option (toggle between list and timeline):
   - Timeline shows snapshots as points on a horizontal bar.
   - Each point shows the snapshot name and date.
   - Below each point, a mini summary: 'N succeeded, M failed'.
   - Clicking a point selects that snapshot in the main view.

4. Add an 'Export History' button that exports all snapshots for a plan (or full lineage) as a single JSON file, useful for documentation or sharing.

Commit.

name: plan-templates-from-history
title: Generate reusable plan templates from completed plan history
prompt:
Allow users to create reusable plan templates from successfully completed plans, so common workflows can be repeated.

1. Create src/orchestrator/ui/template_dialog.py:
   - 'Save as Template' mode: takes a completed plan, strips results and resets statuses to pending, lets the user edit step prompts to make them more generic (replace project-specific details with placeholders like {project_name}, {language}).
   - 'Create from Template' mode: lists saved templates, lets the user pick one, fill in placeholder values, set project path, and create a new plan.

2. In database.py, add a `plan_templates` table:
   ```sql
   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS plan_templates (
       id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
       name TEXT NOT NULL,
       description TEXT,
       steps_json TEXT NOT NULL,
       created_from_plan_id TEXT,
       created_at TEXT NOT NULL
   );
   ```

3. Add CRUD methods: create_template, get_templates, get_template, delete_template.

4. In main.py:
   - Add File > 'Save Plan as Template' (enabled when a plan with succeeded steps is selected).
   - Add File > 'New Plan from Template'.
   - In the New Plan dialog, add a 'Start from Template' button that opens the template picker.

5. When creating from a template, if the template was derived from a plan that still exists, optionally link parent_plan_id for history continuity.

Commit.

name: ux-improvements
title: Polish usability: plan status overview, keyboard shortcuts, and quick actions
prompt:
Improve the overall usability of the orchestrator with quality-of-life enhancements.

1. Plan list improvements (left pane):
   - Show a status badge next to each plan: a colored dot or icon indicating overall status (all-succeeded = green, has-failures = red, in-progress = yellow, not-started = gray).
   - Show step count summary inline: e.g. '5/8 completed'.
   - Add a search/filter Entry above the plan list to filter plans by name.
   - Show parent plan lineage as indented items or with a '↳' prefix.

2. Keyboard shortcuts:
   - Ctrl+N: New Plan
   - Ctrl+E: Extend current plan
   - Ctrl+R: Run Queue
   - Escape: Stop execution
   - Ctrl+H: Open History viewer
   - Delete: Delete selected step (with confirmation)
   - Ctrl+Up/Down: Move step up/down
   - F5: Refresh all

3. Quick actions toolbar:
   - Add a toolbar below the menu bar with icon-style buttons: New Plan, Extend Plan, Run Queue, Stop, History, Settings.
   - Use Unicode characters as pseudo-icons (▶ Run, ⏹ Stop, ➕ New, 📋 History, ⚙ Settings).

4. Step queue improvements:
   - Show a progress bar during queue execution (ttk.Progressbar).
   - After execution completes, show a summary toast/notification: 'Plan completed: 8/10 steps succeeded'.
   - Double-clicking the result pane opens a full-screen result viewer.

5. Status bar improvements:
   - Show total cost (sum of cost_usd from agent_runs) for the current plan.
   - Show elapsed time during execution.
   - Show history snapshot count.

Commit.

name: tests-and-cleanup
title: Add comprehensive tests for all new features and clean up
prompt:
Add tests for all the new functionality and do a final cleanup pass.

1. In tests/, add or extend:
   - tests/test_history.py: Test plan_history CRUD, snapshot creation (verify steps_json content), lineage traversal (parent chain), cascading deletes, auto-snapshot after queue run.
   - tests/test_history_context.py: Test build_history_context with various scenarios: no history, single snapshot, multiple snapshots, lineage, truncation behavior.
   - tests/test_history_tool.py: Test write_history_file creates valid JSON, test cleanup removes the file, test inject_claude_md_hint appends and cleans up correctly.
   - tests/test_templates.py: Test template CRUD, creation from plan, placeholder detection.
   - tests/test_derive_plan.py: Test plan derivation sets parent_plan_id correctly, reference steps are created properly, execution skips reference steps.

2. Update existing tests to handle new fields (parent_plan_id on Plan, REFERENCE status).

3. Run all tests and fix any failures.

4. Clean up:
   - Remove any dead code or unused imports across all modified files.
   - Make sure .gitignore includes .orchestrator-history.json.
   - Update CLAUDE.md with the new architecture (history system, templates, plan lineage).

Commit.
